entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, the crop being studied for drought resistance

entities.name: DROUGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand drought stress

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A method developed to determine drought resistance in chickpea germplasm

entities.name: RATING SCALE
entities.description: A scale from 1-9 used to discard susceptible lines

entities.name: TEL HAYDA
entities.description: A site in northern Syria where the study was conducted

entities.name: NORTHERN SYRIA
entities.description: The region in Syria where Tel Hayda is located

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL CHARACTERS
entities.description: Physical characteristics used for evaluation of chickpea genotypes

entities.name: PHENOLOGICAL CHARACTERS
entities.description: Timing of growth stages used for evaluation of chickpea genotypes

entities.name: SEED CHARACTERS
entities.description: Characteristics of the seeds used for evaluation of chickpea genotypes

entities.name: DELAYED SOWING
entities.description: A specific part of the screening technique involving sowing 3 weeks later during spring

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Stress condition characterized by low water availability

entities.name: SUPPLEMENTAL IRRIGATION
entities.description: Non-stress condition providing additional water to the crop

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced per unit area

entities.name: MEDITERRANEAN REGION
entities.description: The geographical area where the resistant lines are being used

entities.name: ICARDA
entities.description: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the crop to resist diseases

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The ability of the crop to produce a large amount of seed

relationships.description: location where the technique was tested
